scenario: Ironwood Bank ships an updated Camunda 8.8 invoice approval process every 2 weeks with about 250 production invoices in flight at any time. A senior tester is deciding how big the single happy-path test method should be and how it should relate to dedicated tests for the rejection, timer, and archive-error chunks.
question: Which structuring rule from the Camunda 8.8 best practices best fits this test layout?

options.text: Author one large happy-path test method that drives the whole process end to end with a consistent data flow, then add separate dedicated test methods for the rejected, timer-approved, and archive-error chunks of the same process
Author one small focused happy-path test method per service task, and rely on a single combined test method for all deviation paths so the suite stays compact and easy to maintain over time
Author the happy-path coverage exclusively as an integration test against a near-production cluster and keep process tests only for deviations such as the timer and the archive error
Author one parameterized test method per process release that exercises every path in a single run, so each release contributes exactly one test method to the suite as it grows over time

a.text: Correct. The Testing your process in chunks section recommends fully testing the happy path in one big test method to give one consistent data flow, then adding dedicated test methods for the other important chunks such as rejection, timer-driven approval, and archive error.

b.text: Incorrect. Splitting the happy path per service task contradicts the recommended one consistent data flow in one big method.

c.text: Incorrect. The happy path is part of the process-test layer with the same chunk-based strategy; integration tests are an additional verification on top.

d.text: Incorrect. The best practice does not describe a one-method-per-release parameterized approach; it organizes tests by paths through the model.

explanation: Camunda 8.8 testing best practice keeps the happy path in one big end-to-end test method, then adds dedicated test methods for each important chunk such as rejection, timer-driven approval, and archive error.
